narrative:
Harry Souttar is a Centre-Back for the United Kingdom. His overall rating is 7.11/10, placing him in the top 6% of centre-backs at this tournament. This rating reflects his consistent play, with 74% derived from club form over the last two seasons.

Souttar demonstrates strong defensive positioning, evidenced by his 5.769 clearances and 0.923 interceptions per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His attacking output is minimal, with expected goals per 90 minutes at 0.009 and expected assists at 0.014. These numbers highlight his primary role as a defensive anchor, not a creative force.

Souttar is a crucial defensive presence for the United Kingdom, tasked with protecting their goal. Scouts should continue to observe his dominant aerial presence and positional awareness in upcoming matches. Fans can expect him to maintain his role as a reliable backline organizer.
